name: Cepheid1s
description: Red eFRET voltage indicator built by inserting the photostable RFP donor mRuby4 into the first extracellular loop of Ace(D81C) rhodopsin; the more photostable (but dimmer) member of the Cepheid1 pair, enabling long-duration one-photon voltage imaging.

spectrum.name: Cepheid1s
spectrum.note: Cepheid1s uses the RFP donor mRuby4 (an engineered mRuby3 variant; Bajar et al. 2016) inserted into ECL1 of Ace(D81C) rhodopsin. Table S5 reports the Cepheid1s/mRuby4 excitation max 558 nm and emission max 592 nm (561 nm laser, 630/75 emission filter). The paper provides no measured mRuby4 spectrum figure (Fig. S2 only shows mScarlet-I/I1.4, the Cepheid1b donor), and mRuby4 is not in FPbase. mRuby4 is spectrally essentially identical to its parent mRuby3 (identical reported peaks 558/592), so the displayed excitation/emission is the FPbase mRuby3 curve verbatim — the same curve used on the VARNAM page (mRuby3 donor).

kinetics.note: Cepheid1s in HEK293T cells (room temperature, 1058 Hz frame rate). Fig. S7B is a dual-axis bar chart: depolarization (-70 to +30 mV) is read on the LEFT red axis (0-2 ms) and repolarization (+30 to -70 mV) on the RIGHT blue axis (0-3 ms). Pixel-calibrated read: depol t½ ≈ 1.41 ms (the Solaris paper, same lab, confirms depolarizing τ1/2 = 1.5 ± 0.2 ms) and repol t½ ≈ 2.42 ms; Cepheid1s is faster than Cepheid1b in both directions. Converted to time constants via τ = t½/ln2 = 1.443·t½ → on 2.02 ms, off 3.49 ms. (A previous read of off=3.03 ms came from mis-applying a 1.5× left:right axis ratio; the true ratio is ~1.69×.)

Independent comparator measurement in the Solaris paper (Yang et al. 2024, bioRxiv; same Zou lab): Cepheid1s voltage-clamp response half-time τ1/2 = 1.5 ± 0.2 ms in HEK293T cells at 1058 Hz frame rate (main text; measured alongside Solaris, Voltron2585 and Cepheid1b). A single instrument-limited response half-time is reported (not separate depol/repol), converted to a time constant via τ = t½/ln2 = 1.443·t½ → on ≈ 2.16 ms. Consistent with the depolarizing τ from Fig. S7B of the origin paper (2.02 ms). No repolarization (off) half-time given in the Solaris comparison, so off is omitted for this entry.

apWidthData.sample: cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM derived by digitizing the averaged Cepheid1s optical fluorescence waveform (red trace, ΔF/F vs time) in Fig. 1D of the origin paper (20-ms scale bar). Baseline taken from the flat pre-spike segment, peak as the max downward deflection; FWHM measured at the half baseline-to-peak level ≈ 7.9 ms. Recorded in current-clamp cultured rat hippocampal neurons under widefield 1P imaging at 484 Hz (Methods); the frame period (~2.1 ms) is well below the width, so the value is kinetics-dominated (consistent with Cepheid1s τoff ≈ 3.5 ms, slightly narrower than Cepheid1b's ~8.4 ms). No numeric FWHM is stated in the paper; this is plot-derived (this same Fig. 1D panel was cross-digitized when curating the sibling Cepheid1b page).

dynamicRangeData.note: Cepheid1s sensitivity per 100 mV step (-70 to +30 mV) in HEK293T cells under whole-cell voltage clamp (widefield 1P, 1058 Hz); Fig. S7A bar mean ≈30.5%. The main text/Fig. 1B value of -33.6 ± 1.5% (n = 8 cells) is the wider -70 to +40 mV step (the F-V curves are normalized to fluorescence at Vm = 70 mV) and is overstated for a 100 mV window.

sensitivityData.note: Cepheid1s -13.3 ± 0.7% per AP in cultured rat hippocampal neurons (current clamp, widefield 1P imaging at 484 Hz); slightly more sensitive per AP than Cepheid1b (-12.1 ± 0.8%).

brightnessData.note: Cepheid1s average photon count ~250 a.u. vs VARNAM ~580 a.u. per cell under wide-field 561 nm epifluorescence (Fig. S5); Cepheid1s (mRuby4 donor) is the dimmer member of the Cepheid1 pair (Cepheid1b ~960 a.u.), trading brightness for photostability.

photostabilityData.note: Cepheid1s (mRuby4 donor) normalized intensity ≈78% remaining at 13 min under 1.59 W/cm² (= 15.9 mW/mm²) 561 nm wide-field illumination (top curve in Fig. S6A; n = 7). The main text reports a half-photobleach time of more than 13 min for the mRuby4 insertion under 561 nm at 1.6 W/cm² (fig. S6). Cepheid1s is the more photostable member of the pair (Cepheid1b ≈38% remaining at 13 min under the same conditions).

photobleach.note: Cepheid1s (mRuby4 donor) photobleaching digitized from Fig. S6A under 1.59 W/cm² (= 15.9 mW/mm²) 561 nm wide-field illumination (HEK293T, n = 7). HSV colour-separated (Cepheid1s = the absolute topmost dark-brown curve, the most photostable member; ≈77% remaining at 13 min, well above the gold Cepheid1b curve which ends ≈38%). The curve does NOT fall to 75% within the 13-min (765 s) digitized window (it ends at F ≈ 0.768), so t₇₅% is not reached in-window: the honest lower bound is t₇₅% > 765 s. The paper states only a half-photobleach time (t₅₀) > 13 min for the mRuby4 insertion — a t₅₀ lower bound, not a t₇₅% anchor — so t₇₅%/t₅₀ are omitted rather than extrapolated (per the no-unanchored-extrapolation rule). The stored biexponential fit (fast a=0.027/τ₁≈2.1 s + slow τ₂≈3647 s, r²≈0.963) captures the small initial drop then slow decline and, extended, crosses 75% near ~948 s — quoted only as an estimate. Companion curves cross-overlay via the shared source + Fig. S6A.

researchPapers.title: Bright and sensitive red voltage indicators for imaging action potentials in brain slices and pancreatic islets
researchPapers.authors: Han Y, Yang J, Li Y, Chen Y, Ren H, Ding R, Qian W, Ren K, Xie B, Deng M, Xiao Y, Chu J, Zou P
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells, in vitro; rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro (Cepheid1s/Cepheid1s-ST characterized for sensitivity, kinetics, brightness, photostability, and used for all-optical electrophysiology with CheRiff and long-term recording)

researchPapers.title: Solaris: a panel of bright and sensitive hybrid voltage indicators for imaging membrane potential in cultured neurons
researchPapers.authors: Yang J, Zhu S, Yang L, Peng L, Han Y, Hayward RF, Park P, Hu D, Cohen AE, Zou P
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells, in vitro (Cepheid1s measured as a comparator: voltage-clamp response half-time τ1/2 = 1.5 ± 0.2 ms at 1058 Hz)
